title: Bridge Paper: Schizophrenia and the bodily self.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Physical De-solidification** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Body illusion — multisensory integration, rubber hand illusion, ownership as constructed percept.
**Phase context:** body representation, rubber hand illusion, multisensory integration, body ownership, bodily self

Paper: Schizophrenia and the bodily self.
DOI: 10.1016/j.schres.2024.05.014
Abstract: Despite the historically consolidated psychopathological perspective, on the one hand, contemporary organicistic psychiatry often highlights abnormalities in neurotransmitter systems like dysregulation of dopamine transmission, neural circuitry, and genetic factors as key contributors to schizophrenia. Neuroscience, on the other, has so far almost entirely neglected the first-person experiential dimension of this syndrome, mainly focusing on high-order cognitive functions, such as executive func
